description:
Congressman Andrew Clyde (GA-09) seeks students or recent graduates for a full-time internship in his Washington, D.C. office.

Internships are offered year-round for students and recent graduates. Internships are in-person. Hours are flexible to accommodate students' course schedules, but generally run 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Credit hours may be offered to college students.

Interns will have access to Congressional Staff Academy and Congressional Research Service classes and seminars. Interns will also be given opportunities to attend various receptions and networking events while gaining invaluable congressional experience.

This internship is an excellent opportunity for those interested in working politics, policy, or public service.
responsibilities: Completing other various tasks and projects as assigned
qualifications: Excellent oral and written communication skills
Research skills
Friendly and professional demeanor
Team player mentality
Ability to take initiative
Demonstrated eagerness to learn about the legislative process and gain congressional experience
how_to_apply: Please email a resume and cover letter to peter.wright@mail.house.gov with subject line "Fall 2022 Internship Application".

description: The office of Rep. Rodney Davis is seeking fall 2022 interns for the DC office. Motivated individuals should be eager to work and ready to help with various projects around the office. Interns will have the opportunity to learn about Congress and our government throughout their time here.
responsibilities: Help with various projects around the office
Learn about Congress and the government
qualifications: Motivated individual
Eager to work
how_to_apply: To learn more and apply, follow the link to the site: https://rodneydavis.house.gov/internship-application. Or, email kevin.cheng@mail.house.gov your resume and cover letter.

office: Democratic Staff of the House Committee on Oversight and Reform
description:
The Democratic staff of the House Committee on Oversight and Reform is seeking energetic and self-motivated law students for full-time or part-time internships for the spring of 2023.

The position offers current law students the opportunity to gain substantive experience on an exciting and fast-paced Committee, while participating directly in the oversight process and learning about the day-to-day administrative operations of the House of Representatives.

Background: The Oversight and Reform Committee is the primary investigative committee of the U.S. House of Representatives, with jurisdiction to investigate any matter at any time.

Law students play a key role in the office and will work closely with Committee counsel and staff.
responsibilities: Assisting Committee staff in preparing for congressional hearings
Attending various briefings and meetings with Executive-branch and private sector officials for the purpose of taking notes
qualifications: Interest in public service
Desire to learn
Superior attention to detail
Ability to work well under pressure
Self-motivated
Excellent written and oral communication skills
Willingness to work in the office full time (preferred)
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should apply to this position by emailing a cover letter and resume to oversightjobs@mail.house.gov, with "Spring 2023 Legal Internship" in the subject line. Please specify in your cover letter whether you can work full time and when you can start. Please use the following formats to name your resume and cover letter: "LastName.FirstName.Resume" and "LastName.FirstName.Cover Letter." No phone calls or drop-ins please.
equal_opportunity: The office is an equal opportunity employer and encourages candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply.

description:
U.S. Representative David N. Cicilline (D-RI) is seeking a remote Legal Intern for fall 2022 to work with his legislative team.

Interns will work closely with the office's Legislative Counsel and other staff, and duties will entail conducting legal research, drafting materials related to hearings and votes, reviewing and analyzing legislation, attending briefings, and providing other assistance as needed. Opportunities to come into the office for specific events may be available. Issue areas will include constitutional law, oversight over federal agencies, criminal justice, gun violence prevention, voting rights, immigration, intellectual property, privacy, financial services, and women's issues/reproductive rights.

This internship is open to rising 2L and 3L law students (2L or 3L students in the 2022-2023 academic year) in good academic standing. This internship must be done for academic credit. The time commitment is approximately 15 hours per week. Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, individuals with disabilities, and those with Rhode Island ties are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Draft materials related to hearings and votes
qualifications: Rising 2L or 3L law student in the 2022-2023 academic year
Good academic standing
Must receive academic credit for internship
Availability to commit approximately 15 hours per week
how_to_apply: Email a resume, cover letter, three-to-five-page writing sample, unofficial law school transcript, and list of three references with email contacts to RI01LegalInterns@gmail.com. Send all application documents in a single PDF file and include 'Legal Intern Application' in the subject line.
equal_opportunity: Women, people of color, LGBTQ+ individuals, individuals with disabilities, and those with Rhode Island ties are strongly encouraged to apply.

office: House Committee on the Budget (Republican Staff)
description: The Republican Staff of the House Committee on the Budget seeks motivated, reliable, and detail-oriented undergrad students and recent graduates for the Fall Internship Program.
responsibilities: Assisting staff in preparing for congressional hearings
qualifications: Detail oriented
Possess strong oral and written communication skills
Have a strong appreciation for situational awareness
Able to multitask
Adaptable to a fast-paced environment
Interest in topics relevant to the committee's jurisdiction is preferred
how_to_apply: Please submit your resume, cover letter, and one-page writing sample to Carl.Griffin@mail.house.gov

description: Congressman Carlos A. Gimenez is currently seeking full-time interns for his Fall 2022 internship program in Washington, D.C.
responsibilities: Answer and log constituent phone calls
Lead constituent tours of the U.S. Capitol
Assist staff with legislative research and administrative duties
qualifications: Miami ties and Spanish language skills are preferred but not required
how_to_apply: Please send a resume to Gimenez.resume@mail.house.gov to apply.

description:
Representative Susie Lee was sworn in to serve Nevada's third congressional district during the 116th Congress on January 3, 2019, and was re-elected for a second term for the 117th Congress. Rep. Lee serves Nevada's third congressional district in southern Nevada that includes parts of Las Vegas, Henderson, Boulder City, Laughlin, Jean, Goodsprings, and Primm.

Rep. Lee serves on the House Committee on Appropriations, including three subcommittee appointments: Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies; Interior and Environment, and Related Agencies; and Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies. Prior to her being elected to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives, Rep. Lee served the people of Nevada through her work as a nonprofit leader in the education community.

As a district office intern, you would assist with work that aids in the success of the above described role. The role would include, but is not limited to, writing program book letters, writing gold bond congratulatory letters, searching for community events, attending events, presenting certificates at events, making remarks at events, shadowing outreach representatives as they engage in casework, and the chance to create your own event for the Congresswoman.
responsibilities: Shadow outreach representatives as they engage in casework
Create your own event for the Congresswoman
qualifications: Ability to write program book and congratulatory letters
Willingness to attend community events and present certificates
Ability to shadow outreach representatives on casework
Interest in creating events for the Congresswoman
how_to_apply: Submit your resume to NV03resumes@gmail.com. Applicants can also call our office at (702) 963-9336 for more information.

description:
Congressman Cliff Bentz (OR-02) is seeking Fall interns for his Washington D.C. Office. Congressman Bentz is a member of the House Judiciary Committee and the House Natural Resources Committee. Ideal candidates will be college students or recent graduates who are outgoing, proactive, hard-working, detail-oriented, enthusiastic, able to multi-task, and have customer service experience.

Interns will assist with many of the daily activities related to constituent relations and legislative research. Although no two days are the same, an intern may be expected to answer phones, greet Oregonians, attend policy meetings with other staff, and assist in managing constituent correspondences and administrative operations. Interns may also lead tours of the U.S. Capitol and attend committee hearings.

Interns will be immersed in the congressional process and their work will provide valuable experience for future endeavors. The internship will generate the opportunity to foster excellent connections, learn about many issues affecting our state and country, gain expertise in any policy areas you might be interested in, and get a detailed look at what makes the United States the most enduring democracy in the world.

Desired Availability: Applicants should be able to commit to at least 20 hours per week. Office hours are 9:00AM - 5:00PM weekdays. The internship is in-person.
responsibilities: Assist with daily activities related to constituent relations and legislative research
Attend policy meetings with other staff
Assist in managing constituent correspondences and administrative operations
Lead tours of the U.S. Capitol
qualifications: College students or recent graduates
Outgoing, proactive, hard-working, detail-oriented, and enthusiastic
Able to multi-task
Customer service experience
Able to commit to at least 20 hours per week
Available weekdays between 9:00AM - 5:00PM
Able to work in-person
how_to_apply: All interested applicants are asked to fill out the application form found on the Congressman's website: https://bentz.house.gov/services/internships or email resumes to Natalia.Vasquez@mail.house.gov with the subject line: "Fall 2022 Internship".

description:
The Office of Rep. Eric Swalwell (CA-15) seeks a press fellow for the upcoming Fall 2022 term from September to December.

Fellows are eligible for a stipend. The ideal candidate will have strong written and oral communication skills, familiarity with graphic design including Canva and Adobe creative suite, and relevant internship experience related to communications, government, or campaigns.

Duties include assisting with editing video clips, creating digital content, developing social media content and helping the team with a variety of projects as needed throughout the week. A strong work ethic, the ability to excel in fast-paced environments, and a willingness to assist wherever needed is a must.

Hours are flexible to accommodate students' course schedules; generally, a full-time fellowship would run from 9:00AM to 5:00PM ET, at least three days a week.
responsibilities: Help the team with a variety of projects as needed throughout the week
qualifications: Strong written and oral communication skills
Familiarity with graphic design including Canva and Adobe Creative Suite
Relevant internship experience related to communications, government, or campaigns
Strong work ethic
Ability to excel in fast-paced environments
Willingness to assist wherever needed
how_to_apply: To apply please email resume, cover letter, and two examples of previous digital media or graphic design products to Jessica.Gail@mail.house.gov with "Press Fellow" in the subject line.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, sex, color, age, religion, disability, national origin, uniformed status, sexual orientation, or gender identity or expression.

description:
The Washington, D.C. Office of Congressman Steve Cohen (D-TN-09) is seeking interns for fall 2022. This is a hybrid position - hours are worked remotely and in-person. A majority of the program is spent in the office.

This position offers an opportunity to learn about the U.S. House of Representatives and gain substantive work experience in an exciting and fast-paced office. Internships are offered on a full-time and part-time basis, and stipends are offered based on need. Full-time availability is preferred. Ties to Tennessee's 9th District are preferred but not required. Start dates are flexible with Interns will be working for approximately 10-16 weeks (August - November). Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis.
responsibilities: Assist with constituent correspondence and draft email blasts and other forms of outreach
Attend virtual briefings and track virtual hearings
Conduct legislative research on an as-needed basis
qualifications: Demonstrated interest in politics, policy and/or public service
Strong communications and writing skills
Hard-working, dependable, detail-oriented and professional
Self-motivated (especially given that all work is remote)
Ties to Tennessee's 9th District are a bonus, but not required
how_to_apply:
To apply, applicants must complete the form and submit the following materials on https://cohen.house.gov/services/internships:

- Resume
- Cover letter
- Three references
salary_info: Stipends are offered based on need
